structured.intro: Vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz have collapsed to **6/day from a 110/day baseline (−95%)** following the **May 13 sinking of the HAJI ALI** and escalating Iranian military operations, with **16 dark vessel detections in 24 hours** and major carriers including **Maersk, MSC, Hapag-Lloyd, and CMA CGM** suspending operations. War risk premiums on VLCCs and LR2s are unquotable after the last quote of **5.2% hull value**, while AG–East VLCC freight rates have spiked to **$172k/day (6x the $28k baseline)**, signaling acute capacity scarcity and forcing shippers toward pipeline bypass routes operating at **62% utilization on the East-West Pipeline**.
